behavior_summary: U8P_SKULCAND::look is a simple 'U8P_SKULCAND::look' (examine) handler for a skull-candle object. When the player inspects the object it displays a single short player-facing text/bark: "skull candle ".
Immediately after showing the bark the process suspends/terminates, so no further interaction or branching occurs.
No global flags are read or written, and there are no external function calls or menu/dialogue interactions.
notable_control_flow: Entry on a U8P_SKULCAND::look/examine event (process form, processType 0x0208).
Single output action: emit a Bark line containing the text "skull candle ".
Followed by a suspend/terminate of the U8P_SKULCAND::look process; linear flow, no conditionals or loops.
open_questions: The bark text contains a trailing space ("skull candle "). Is this intentional or an artifact of extraction/encoding?
The disassembly shows two Unknown nodes alongside the Bark node; do they encode any additional side effects (e.g., logging or analytics) not visible in the structured output?

main_function.name: U8P_SKULCAND::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description shown to the player when they examine the skull-candle item; display the single bark text and end the U8P_SKULCAND::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player performs a 'U8P_SKULCAND::look' (examine) action on the skull-candle object (processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_SKULCAND::look
function_map.role: Item U8P_SKULCAND::look/examine handler that emits a single descriptive bark and then suspends/ends the process.
